steps.title: Identify what the blank must supply
steps.content: The blank precedes "112th in a global ranking of the shortest constitutions." That predicate needs a subject plus a linking verb to form a complete sentence. The referent is "the constitution of Poland" — singular.

steps.title: Apply the contraction vs. possessive rule — per-choice diagnosis
steps.content:
The blank needs a subject-verb pair ("it is"). Four choices look similar but function differently.
  • Choice A — its possessive pronoun. Has no verb; "its 112th" would mean the constitution owns the ranking, which is nonsensical here. Eliminated.
  • Choice B — they're contraction of "they are" (plural). The constitution is singular, so "they're" creates a number mismatch. Eliminated.
  • Choice C — it's contraction of "it is" — singular subject + linking verb. Supplies exactly what the sentence needs to be grammatically complete. Correct.
  • Choice D — their plural possessive pronoun. Neither matches the singular referent nor provides a verb. Eliminated.
